Dept.505: Judge: Cecillia Castellanos; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:07:04 a.m. and concluding at 9:34:28 a.m. (D505)

Kimberly Chu, Esq. representing Plaintiffs, wrongful death heirs of James Stuart, Deceased via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Rae Stuart, successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of James Stuart, Deceased was placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Christopher Stuart as wrongful death heir of James Stuart, Deceased was placed under oath via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Castle Bryant as wrongful death heir of James Stuart, Deceased not appearing.

Plaintiff Christopher Setters as wrongful death heir of James Stuart, Deceased was placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Cameron Setters as wrongful death heir of James Stuart, Deceased was placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.

The Court comments on the motion of the Plaintiff and inquires as to why this motion is done now as to later as there are plans to pursue other Defendants for recovery.
Counsel offers that there is a settlement in the amount of $34,116.66 that is available and ready for distribution among the Plaintiffs.
The matter is discussed between Court and Counsel.

Plaintiff Rae Stuart is examined by the Court.
Plaintiff Rae Stuart testifies and prefers Plaintiff Christopher Stuart as signatory on behalf of other plaintiffs. Plaintiff requests to be excluded from any and all settlement. Plaintiff Rae Stuart confirms with the Court that she understands that she will not receive any monetary settlement resulting from this case.

Plaintiff Christopher Setters is examined by the Court and testifies that he is amicable to what the Court decides.

Plaintiff Cameron Setters is examined by the Court and testifies that he is amicable to what the Court decides.

After hearing the testimony and considering the declarations, The Court grants and apportions the distribution of funds as follows:

RULING:
Not contested, not argued, and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is June 5, 2023 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is June 16, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 1, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 19, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 2, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 16, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
